The deep teal glaze has a mirror-like quality â€” glossy, rich, and full of movement. In direct light it reads as a vivid jewel-tone teal; in shade it deepens to near-emerald. The minimalist black metal ring keeps the visual focus on the pottery and your plants, creating a floating, sculptural effect. Whether you fill it with a lush fern, a dramatic spider plant, or a simple trailing ivy, this planter elevates everything it holds.

name: Celadon Drip-Glaze Vase
description: Soft, serene, and effortlessly elegant â€” this celadon vase is a masterclass in understated beauty. The pale seafoam-green body has a calming, almost jade-like quality, while the white crawl glaze cascading from the rim creates an organic, lace-like texture that catches the light in unexpected ways. The crawl effect happens naturally during firing as the glaze contracts and separates, leaving a raised, crater-like pattern that's mesmerizing up close. Standing approximately 7 inches tall with a classic curved silhouette, this vase is perfectly proportioned for a generous bouquet of tulips, a spray of wildflowers, or a simple arrangement of greenery. Shown here with vibrant yellow tulips on a warm wood dining table â€” the contrast between the cool celadon and bright flowers is absolutely stunning.

The matching navy saucer with a subtle gloss finish completes the look and protects your surfaces. At approximately 5.5 inches tall, it's the perfect size for a statement desk plant â€” a money tree, a compact fiddle leaf, or a lush pothos. name: Navy Square Planter with Saucer
description: Bold geometry meets organic glaze art in this square-form planter that commands attention from every angle. The architectural silhouette â€” clean lines, flat facets, a perfectly square footprint â€” provides a structured canvas for the wild, unpredictable cascade of bubbly sage-green and amber glaze that flows over the navy body. The contrast between the precise shape and the organic glaze movement creates a visual tension that's endlessly interesting. Each of the four faces catches the drip glaze differently, meaning you can rotate the planter to show a different 'painting' depending on your mood. The matching square navy saucer completes the geometric theme. Shown here in a vibrant garden surrounded by pink kalanchoe and daffodils, this planter is equally at home on a modern desk or a cottage garden shelf.
